name: zig
description: Idiomatic Zig conventions, allocator patterns, error handling, comptime, testing, and build system practices

decisions.decision: Target the latest stable Zig release; set minimum_zig_version in build.zig.zon
decisions.rationale: Zig is pre-1.0 with breaking changes between minor versions. Targeting the latest stable ensures access to critical fixes and API improvements. minimum_zig_version documents compatibility (advisory only — not yet enforced by the compiler).
decisions.details: Check ziglang.org/download/ for current stable. Use master builds for experimentation only. Expect breaking migrations (e.g., 0.14->0.15: I/O overhaul, ArrayList changes, usingnamespace removal) until 1.0.

decisions.decision: Follow the official Zig style guide: PascalCase for types and type-returning functions, camelCase for regular functions, snake_case for variables and fields
decisions.rationale: Zig naming conventions encode semantic meaning. When you see a PascalCase function name, you immediately know it returns a type. This is a powerful signal embedded in naming that the community universally follows.
decisions.details: Types: PascalCase. Type-returning fns: PascalCase. Regular fns: camelCase. Vars/fields/consts: snake_case. Namespace structs: snake_case binding. Files exposing type: PascalCase (HttpClient.zig). Regular files: snake_case (main.zig). zig fmt enforces whitespace only, not naming.

decisions.decision: Use explicit error sets for public APIs, inferred error sets for internals; errdefer for all partial cleanup
decisions.rationale: Explicit error sets document what can go wrong and enable exhaustive handling at call sites. Inferred error sets reduce boilerplate in internal code. errdefer is essential for correct cleanup when functions allocate multiple resources — it only runs when the function returns an error.
decisions.details:
Use try to propagate, catch for custom handling, errdefer for error-path-only cleanup.
Patterns: errdefer allocator.free(memory); errdefer |err| log.err("failed: {}", .{err}); errdefer comptime unreachable (assert no error path).
Avoid: catch unreachable (unless proven), catch {} (silent swallow), over-broad error sets.

decisions.decision: Always pass allocators as parameters; use DebugAllocator in development, ArenaAllocator for batch work, std.testing.allocator in tests
decisions.rationale: Explicit allocator injection is Zig's central design pattern — no hidden control flow. When you see an allocator parameter, the function will allocate. Different allocators serve different purposes: DebugAllocator detects leaks and UAF, ArenaAllocator enables efficient bulk allocation, std.testing.allocator automatically reports leaks.
decisions.details:
DebugAllocator (dev), SmpAllocator (prod MT), ArenaAllocator (batch), std.testing.allocator (tests), FixedBufferAllocator (embedded), c_allocator (C interop, -lc).
defer allocator.free() right after alloc. Slices: alloc/free. Singles: create/destroy.
Avoid: globals, free() on arena (no-op), page_allocator for small allocs.

decisions.decision: Use zig init for project scaffolding; build.zig for build instructions, build.zig.zon for package metadata
decisions.rationale: zig init creates the canonical project structure. build.zig is Zig code (not a DSL) providing full build system expressiveness. build.zig.zon is the package manifest with dependencies, version, and fingerprint.
decisions.details:
Layout: build.zig, build.zig.zon, src/main.zig (exe entry), src/root.zig (lib root), src/Module.zig (PascalCase = exports type). Gitignore .zig-cache/ and zig-out/.
Every .zig file IS a struct; @import returns its struct type (files as namespaces/types).
Package name: bare Zig identifier, max 32 bytes, no 'zig' prefix.

decisions.decision: Use build.zig.zon for dependencies with zig fetch --save; no central registry — use direct URLs
decisions.rationale: Zig's package manager is decentralized. Packages are distributed via Git URLs and tarballs. The hash in build.zig.zon IS the lock — no separate lockfile needed. Transitive dependencies are automatically resolved.
decisions.details:
Add: zig fetch --save <url>. Use: b.dependency("name", .{.target=target, .optimize=optimize}), then exe.root_module.addImport("name", dep.module("name")).
Optional deps: lazy: true in build.zig.zon, access via b.lazyDependency() (returns optional).
Hash is source of truth, not URL. Deps only fetched/built when used.

decisions.decision: Colocate tests with implementation; use std.testing.allocator for all test allocations; use descriptive test names
decisions.rationale: The standard library keeps tests in the same file as the code under test — this is the canonical pattern. std.testing.allocator automatically detects memory leaks after test completion. Descriptive names enable test filtering.
decisions.details:
Forms: test "name" (filterable), test myFunction (doctest, in docs), test {} (unnamed, always runs).
Assertions: expect(), expectEqual(), expectError(), expectEqualStrings(), expectEqualSlices().
Skip: return error.SkipZigTest for platform-specific tests.
Run: zig build test, zig test src/main.zig, zig build test -- --test-filter "name".

decisions.decision: Use comptime for generics, type introspection, and compile-time validation; never for I/O or side effects
decisions.rationale: Comptime is Zig's single mechanism replacing macros, templates, and generics. It is hermetic, reproducible, and cacheable — no I/O or side effects allowed. This makes builds deterministic.
decisions.details:
Generic types: fn ArrayList(comptime T: type) type. Introspection: @typeInfo(), @FieldType(), @tagName(), @field(). inline for for unrolling. comptime assert() for validation. @Type() for dynamic types.
No I/O at comptime; use build.zig for codegen. Avoid comptime when runtime suffices.

decisions.decision: Use ReleaseSafe for production builds; rely on defer/errdefer for cleanup; understand that Zig lacks compile-time borrow checking
decisions.rationale: ReleaseSafe enables runtime bounds checking, overflow detection, and undefined detection while maintaining good performance. Unlike Rust, Zig does not prevent use-after-free or iterator invalidation at compile time — safety relies on runtime checks and disciplined coding.
decisions.details:
Modes: Debug (all checks, 0xAA fill), ReleaseSafe (checks+opt), ReleaseFast (no checks), ReleaseSmall (size).
defer=unconditional, errdefer=error-path, defer assert()=postconditions.
Wrapping: +%, -%, *%. Saturating: +|, -|, *|. DebugAllocator in dev for UAF/double-free.

decisions.decision: Always run zig fmt; trailing commas control single-line vs multi-line layout
decisions.rationale: zig fmt is the canonical AST-based auto-formatter (like gofmt). Zero configuration. All Zig code should be formatted with it. Non-canonical formatting causes unnecessary churn in diffs.
decisions.details:
Run: zig fmt src/ or zig fmt . Trailing comma forces multi-line; remove it to collapse to single-line.
Opt out: // zig fmt: off / // zig fmt: on.
CI: zig fmt --check src/ (non-zero exit on diff).

decisions.decision: Use build.zig with the module-based API; define standard steps for run, test, and install
decisions.rationale: build.zig is Zig code — not a DSL — providing full expressiveness. The module-based API (createModule) is the current standard. Steps run concurrently as a DAG; dependency edges determine execution order.
decisions.details:
b.standardTargetOptions(.{}), b.standardOptimizeOption(.{}), b.addExecutable + b.createModule(.{.root_source_file=b.path("src/main.zig"), ...}), b.installArtifact(). Define run, test, install steps.
Advanced: b.option(), addOptions() (comptime injection), addRunArtifact+captureStdOut (codegen). Use b.path() for sources, LazyPath for generated.

decisions.decision: Document all public APIs with /// doc comments; use //! for module-level documentation; write doctests
decisions.rationale: Zig doc comments support Markdown and are rendered by autodoc. Doctests (test named with an identifier) appear in generated documentation and serve as both docs and tests.
decisions.details:
/// for declaration docs. //! for module-level docs (file top). Doctests: test myFunction { ... } appears in generated docs.
Markdown only, no HTML. Generate: build system or zig build-exe -femit-docs.
